tools.name: create_pcg_graph
tools.description:
Create a new empty PCG (Procedural Content Generation) graph asset.

Requires: Unreal Engine 5.2+ with the PCG plugin enabled in the .uproject file.
Production-ready: UE 5.7+. PCG Editor Mode is experimental — use with caution.

PCG Workflow:
1. create_pcg_graph — create the empty graph asset
2. attach_pcg_component — assign the graph to an actor (e.g. a PCGVolume or Landscape)
3. set_pcg_parameter — configure exposed parameters (mesh references, density, seed)
4. generate_pcg_local — trigger generation
5. get_pcg_info — inspect output

NOTE: PCG graph node authoring (adding scatter/sample/spline/filter nodes and connecting them)
must be done in the PCG Editor UI. AgentZet covers the workflow of creating, assigning,
parameterizing, and generating PCG graphs.

Naming convention: PCG_ prefix (e.g. PCG_ForestBiome, PCG_RockScatter)

asset_path.description: Content path for the PCG graph asset, e.g. /Game/PCG/PCG_ForestBiome

tools.name: attach_pcg_component
tools.description:
Add a UPCGComponent to an actor in the current level and optionally assign a PCG graph.

The actor must already exist in the level (use spawn_actor to create it first if needed).
For large landscape coverage, spawn a 'Volume' actor type and place the PCGComponent on it.

After attaching, use set_pcg_parameter to configure the graph's exposed parameters,
then generate_pcg_local to trigger generation.

The actor_name must match the actor's label as shown in the Outliner.

actor_name.description: Label of the actor in the current level (as shown in the Outliner). Case-sensitive.

graph_path.description: Content path of the PCG graph asset to assign, e.g. /Game/PCG/PCG_ForestBiome. If omitted, adds an empty PCGComponent — assign graph later.

tools.name: set_pcg_parameter
tools.description:
Set an exposed parameter on a PCG component's graph instance.

Parameters must be exposed in the PCG graph settings asset ('Expose to Component' checkbox in the PCG graph editor).

Common use cases:
  - Set a static mesh reference: parameter_name='TreeMesh', parameter_value='/Game/Trees/SM_Pine', parameter_type='object'
  - Set density: parameter_name='Density', parameter_value='0.15', parameter_type='float'
  - Set random seed: parameter_name='Seed', parameter_value='1337', parameter_type='int'
  - Set bounds: parameter_name='SpawnRadius', parameter_value='5000.0', parameter_type='float'

After setting parameters, call generate_pcg_local to apply them.

actor_name.description: Label of the actor owning the PCG component

parameter_name.description: Exact name of the exposed parameter as defined in the PCG graph

parameter_value.description: String-serialized value: '0.15' for float, '1337' for int, 'true'/'false' for bool, '/Game/...' for object reference

parameter_type.description: Type hint for correct serialization: 'float', 'int', 'bool', 'string', 'vector', 'object'. Default: 'string'

tools.name: generate_pcg_local
tools.description:
Trigger local PCG generation on an actor's PCG component.

Calls UPCGComponent::GenerateLocal(bForce) — equivalent to pressing 'Generate' in the editor.
Generation runs asynchronously. Use get_pcg_info after a moment to check output actor count.

Use force=true to regenerate even if the graph state is clean (useful after parameter changes).

actor_name.description: Label of the actor with the PCG component to generate

force.description: Force regeneration even if graph state is clean. Default: true. Always use true after parameter changes.

tools.name: get_pcg_info
tools.description:
Read-only query: return the PCG component state of an actor.

Returns:
  - has_pcg_component: whether the actor has a PCGComponent
  - graph: content path of the assigned PCG graph
  - is_generating: whether generation is currently running

Call this after generate_pcg_local to verify completion. If is_generating is true,
wait briefly and call again.

actor_name.description: Label of the actor in the current level to inspect
